demos.author: Delta Forge Team

demos.name: Delta Unicode Roundtrip — Mutation Fidelity Proof
demos.summary: An international e-commerce marketplace stores product listings in sellers' native scripts — Japanese, Chinese, Korean, Arabic, Hebrew, Cyrillic, and Latin diacritics. Proves UTF-8 roundtrip fidelity by asserting exact Unicode values after every DML mutation: UPDATE on Unicode columns directly, DELETE with CJK LIKE predicates, and copy-on-write Parquet rewrites.
demos.author: Delta Forge Team

demos.name: Delta Unicode String Functions — Multi-Byte Operations
demos.summary: A multilingual customer support platform receives tickets in 15+ languages. Tests whether SQL string functions — LENGTH, UPPER, LOWER, SUBSTR, LIKE, and CONCAT — correctly handle multi-byte UTF-8 characters. LENGTH must return character count (not byte count), SUBSTR must not split multi-byte sequences, and UPPER must preserve diacritics (ü → Ü).
demos.author: Delta Forge Team

demos.name: Delta Cross-Partition Updates
demos.summary: When an UPDATE predicate does not align with the partition key, Delta must create deletion vectors (DVs) in every partition that contains matching rows. This demo uses a SaaS billing platform where pricing tier changes apply globally across all regions, forcing cross-partition DV writes.
demos.author: Delta Forge Team

demos.name: Delta UPDATE Expressions — Portfolio Rebalancing
demos.summary: Showcases computed expressions in UPDATE SET clauses: arithmetic adjustments (equity +5% appreciation), CASE WHEN reclassification (dynamic risk ratings based on value thresholds), and percentage-based recalculations (bond -2% decline). Three investment portfolios with 20 holdings go through a quarterly rebalancing cycle. VERSION AS OF and DESCRIBE HISTORY compare pre- and post-rebalancing snapshots.
demos.author: Delta Forge Team

demos.name: Delta UPDATE Multi-Pass — ETL Pipeline Stages
demos.summary: Sequential UPDATE passes transform 30 e-commerce orders through a 3-stage ETL pipeline: Normalize (UPPER+TRIM status codes), Classify (CASE WHEN priority/shipping assignment), Enrich (total_with_tax and estimated_profit computation). VERSION AS OF inspects data at each pipeline stage, demonstrating how Delta versioning provides built-in pipeline checkpoints.
demos.author: Delta Forge Team

demos.name: Delta UPDATE NULL Backfill — Patient Data Recovery
demos.summary: After a hospital database migration, many fields arrived as NULL or with sentinel values (-999, 'N/A', ''). This demo walks through 8 UPDATE passes using NULLIF, COALESCE, CASE WHEN IS NULL, and conditional SET to replace sentinels with proper NULLs and backfill missing values with defaults across 25 patient records.
demos.author: Delta Forge Team

demos.name: Delta UPDATE String Cleansing — CRM Data Normalization
demos.summary: UPDATE statements with string functions clean messy data in place. Imports 25 customer records from a legacy CRM system — emails in mixed case, names with extra whitespace, unpadded account codes, and missing full names — then walks through 5 UPDATE passes using TRIM, LOWER, LPAD, concatenation (||), and UPPER to normalize every field.
demos.author: Delta Forge Team

demos.name: Delta VACUUM — Cleanup Orphaned Files
demos.summary: VACUUM is a Delta Lake maintenance command that removes data files no longer \ referenced by the current table version, reclaiming storage after UPDATEs, \ DELETEs, and OPTIMIZE operations...
demos.author: Delta Forge Team

demos.name: Delta Maintenance Playbook — OPTIMIZE Then VACUUM
demos.summary: The standard Delta Lake maintenance sequence: OPTIMIZE compacts fragmented small files from daily micro-batch ingestion, then VACUUM RETAIN 0 HOURS purges the orphaned pre-compaction files. Uses an e-commerce order pipeline with DESCRIBE DETAIL at each stage to show physical file state while data integrity stays constant.
demos.author: Delta Forge Team

demos.name: Delta VACUUM Retention Governance — Compliance-Driven Data Lifecycle
demos.summary: Compares two VACUUM retention strategies on a trade settlement table: safe retention (168 hours, preserves all recent versions for audit) versus aggressive cleanup (RETAIN 0 HOURS, destroys time travel). DESCRIBE HISTORY proves the audit trail survives both strategies — only data file availability differs. Governance-first framing for regulated environments.
demos.author: Delta Forge Team

demos.name: Delta VACUUM Storage Diagnostics — Measuring Cleanup Impact
demos.summary: Walks through a product inventory lifecycle — price adjustments, discontinued removals, new arrivals — running DESCRIBE DETAIL at each stage to track version, file count, and byte size. After VACUUM, the same diagnostic queries prove cleanup worked while data remains bit-for-bit identical. The first demo to systematically use DESCRIBE DETAIL for storage auditing.
demos.author: Delta Forge Team

demos.name: Delta VACUUM — Storage Cost Savings
demos.summary: Every UPDATE and DELETE in Delta Lake orphans old Parquet files via copy-on-write, silently inflating cloud storage costs. This demo uses a SaaS billing system to show how DESCRIBE DETAIL measures file accumulation and VACUUM RETAIN 0 HOURS reclaims storage — proving zero data change through before/after integrity checks.
demos.author: Delta Forge Team

demos.name: Delta Views & Data Masking — Role-Based Access Layers
demos.summary: Production databases serve multiple audiences with different access needs. This demo builds three role-based views on an e-commerce order table: an analyst view (no PII), a support view (masked credit cards), and an executive view (pre-aggregated regional summaries). Each view enforces its access boundary at the SQL layer.
demos.author: Delta Forge Team

demos.name: Retail Sales Rep Leaderboard
demos.summary: A retail chain ranks 10 sales reps across 4 quarters (40 rows) using advanced window functions. DENSE_RANK assigns leaderboard positions with tie handling, NTILE splits into performance quartiles, LEAD compares to next-best performer, PERCENT_RANK and CUME_DIST compute relative standing, and ROWS BETWEEN frames smooth revenue trends.
demos.author: Delta Forge Team

demos.name: Delta Z-ORDER — Multi-Column Data Layout Optimization
demos.summary: Z-ORDER reorganizes data files using a space-filling curve to co-locate rows with similar values across multiple columns. This demo builds an IoT telemetry table in 3 batches, uses DESCRIBE DETAIL to observe file layout, applies OPTIMIZE ZORDER BY, then verifies compaction with DESCRIBE HISTORY.
demos.author: Delta Forge Team

demos.name: EDI EDIFACT — International Trade & Logistics
demos.summary: Overview of UN/EDIFACT and EANCOM message parsing across 22 files covering international trade, logistics, customs, and passenger data. Demonstrates 6 syntax identifiers (UNOA through IATA), 17+ distinct message types, domain classification (Commerce/Transport/Border/Acknowledgment), EANCOM vs EDIFACT standard differentiation, and full JSON transaction access.
demos.author: Delta Forge Team

demos.name: EDIFACT Customs & Border Control
demos.summary: Port authority customs system processing UN/EDIFACT messages for border security and trade compliance. Parses cargo manifests (CUSCAR) with consignment and goods item details, passenger lists (PAXLST) with travel document verification, passenger name records (PNRGOV) for advance screening, and bayplan stowage plans (BAPLIE) for vessel operations. Demonstrates materialized_paths for BGM, TDT, NAD, LOC, CNI, GID, EQD, and DOC segments — message types unique to EDIFACT with no X12 equivalent.
demos.author: Delta Forge Team

demos.name: EANCOM Retail Supply Chain
demos.summary: European retailer EDI hub processing GS1/EANCOM messages across the retail supply chain. Covers despatch advices (DESADV) for warehouse receiving, invoices (INVOIC) for accounts payable, order responses (ORDRSP) for procurement, price catalogues (PRICAT) for product master data, transport status (IFTSTA) for shipment tracking, and transport instructions (IFTMIN) for carrier booking. EANCOM is the GS1 subset of EDIFACT used by major European retailers — demonstrates BGM document codes, NAD trading partner identification, LIN product lines with GTINs, and STS shipment status tracking.
demos.author: Delta Forge Team

demos.name: EDIFACT Invoice Reconciliation
demos.summary: Accounts payable reconciliation matching EDIFACT purchase orders (ORDERS) with order responses (ORDRSP) and invoices (INVOIC) across the procurement cycle. Extracts BGM document codes (220=Order, 380/393=Invoice), NAD trading partner identifiers, LIN product line items with EAN codes, DTM date qualifiers, MOA monetary amounts, and TAX details. Two EDIFACT directory versions (D:96A, D:01B) demonstrate cross-version reconciliation with materialized_paths for document matching, amount validation, and partner verification.
demos.author: Delta Forge Team

demos.name: EDI HIPAA Claim Status — Inquiry & Authorization Tracking
demos.summary: Demonstrates HIPAA claim status tracking and prior authorization review using CASE on composite status codes, LIKE pattern matching, and multi-table JOINs. Three transactions cover the 276/277 claim status request/response cycle and 278 health services review. Queries classify composite STC codes (P3:317 = pending, F0:3 = finalized) using LIKE, correlate request amounts with response statuses, and JOIN two specialized tables for unified lifecycle views.
demos.author: Delta Forge Team

demos.name: EDI HIPAA Claims Financial — Charge & Remittance Analysis
demos.summary: Demonstrates financial analysis of HIPAA X12 healthcare claims using SUM, CAST, HAVING, and JOIN — SQL features not covered by any other EDI demo. Four transactions cover 837 professional/dental/institutional claims and 835 remittance advice. Queries compute total charges with SUM, filter with HAVING, calculate write-off percentages with CAST and arithmetic, and JOIN claim charges with remittance payments for end-to-end reconciliation.
demos.author: Delta Forge Team

demos.name: EDI HIPAA Eligibility & Enrollment — Coverage Verification
demos.summary: Demonstrates HIPAA eligibility verification and benefit enrollment analysis using CTEs, JOINs, and COALESCE — SQL patterns unique across all EDI demos. Three transactions cover the 270/271 eligibility request/response cycle and 834 benefit enrollment. A CTE pairs request with response by matching TRN trace numbers, and a JOIN correlates eligibility context with enrollment plan data.
demos.author: Delta Forge Team

demos.name: EDI HIPAA Healthcare — Claims Lifecycle
demos.summary: Demonstrates X12 EDI transaction parsing for the healthcare claims lifecycle. 11 transactions spanning 9 HIPAA 5010 transaction types: eligibility verification (270/271), claim status (276/277), healthcare claims (837 Professional/Dental/Institutional), payment remittance (835), benefit enrollment (834), prior authorization (278), and payment order (820). Covers materialized path extraction and full JSON access.
demos.author: Delta Forge Team

demos.name: EDI X12 Supply Chain — Order-to-Cash Lifecycle
demos.summary: Demonstrates X12 EDI transactions spanning the full supply chain order-to-cash lifecycle. 14 real-world transactions across 8 types: purchase orders (850), invoices (810), PO acknowledgments (855), ship notices (856), shipment & billing (857), receiving advice (861), functional acknowledgment (997), and application advice (824). Multi-version X12 (00204, 00401) with trading partner diversity.
demos.author: Delta Forge Team

demos.name: EDI Compliance Validation — 997/824 Error & Rejection Analysis
demos.summary: Demonstrates EDI compliance monitoring using 997 Functional Acknowledgment and 824 Application Advice transactions. Extracts AK1-AK9 acknowledgment segments, OTI issue codes, and TED error descriptions to track transaction acceptance rates, identify segment-level rejections (TXI segment error, element error code 7), and diagnose application-level failures (invalid part/plant/supplier). The only EDI demo focused on error detection and compliance rather than business data.
demos.author: Delta Forge Team

demos.name: EDI JSON Segment Extraction — On-Demand X12 Deep Access
demos.summary: Demonstrates SQL JSON functions (json_array_length, json_extract_path_text, json_typeof, jsonb_pretty, #>> path operator) on EDI transaction data. Analysts explore and extract any X12 segment on-demand from df_transaction_json without pre-configuring materialized paths — the only EDI demo showcasing JSON function capabilities. Covers segment counting, type inspection, BEG/BIG field extraction via JSON paths, and formatted output.
demos.author: Delta Forge Team

demos.name: EDI Order Lifecycle Tracking — Cross-Document X12 Traceability
demos.summary: Demonstrates cross-document traceability by correlating 8 X12 transaction types in a single unified table. Traces the order-to-cash lifecycle: PO creation (850) → acknowledgment (855) → shipment (856/857) → invoicing (810) → receipt (861). Uses CASE/COALESCE to map document IDs and dates across BEG, BIG, BAK, BSN, and BRA segments. The only EDI demo with cross-document lifecycle queries rather than single-type analysis.
demos.author: Delta Forge Team

demos.name: EDI Repeating Segments — Multi-Occurrence X12 Handling
demos.summary: Demonstrates the repeating_segment_mode EDI option with three tables from the same X12 data: Indexed mode creates per-occurrence columns (n1_1_1, n1_2_1 for first/second N1), Concatenate mode joins all values with pipes (n1_1 = 'ST|BY|SO'), and ToJson mode produces JSON arrays (n1_1 = '["ST","BY","SO"]'). Shows multi-line PO1 extraction, N1 address analysis, and price computation across indexed occurrences.
demos.author: Delta Forge Team

demos.name: EDI TRADACOMS — UK Retail Orders & Utilities
demos.summary: Demonstrates TRADACOMS — the UK-specific EDI standard used in retail and utilities. 4 files containing 15 total messages cover purchase orders, product planning forecasts, and utility bills. Features multi-message file parsing, STX envelope extraction, TRADACOMS escape character decoding (?' and ?+), and EAN-13 product codes.
demos.author: Delta Forge Team

demos.name: EDI TRADACOMS — Purchase Order Line Items
demos.summary: Extracts order line items (OLD segments), delivery instructions, and totals from a UK grocery retailer’s TRADACOMS purchase order. Demonstrates the Header–Detail–Trailer message pattern, cross-message analysis, and materialized_paths for deep segment extraction.
demos.author: Delta Forge Team

demos.name: EDI TRADACOMS — UK Energy Billing & VAT Reconciliation
demos.summary: Extracts billing details from CCD charge segments, verifies VAT calculations, and reconciles bill totals from a UK energy company’s TRADACOMS utility bills. Demonstrates escape character decoding for special characters in customer names.
demos.author: Delta Forge Team

demos.name: EDI TRADACOMS — JSON Deep Segment Extraction
demos.summary: Demonstrates on-demand extraction of deeply nested TRADACOMS segments using SQL JSON functions. Queries product planning forecasts (PDN/PLO/SFS) and order line items (OLD/DIN) directly from df_transaction_json without materialized_paths.
demos.author: Delta Forge Team

demos.name: EDI X12 Transportation — Freight Lifecycle
demos.summary: Demonstrates X12 EDI transactions spanning the full freight lifecycle. 12 files across 8 types: load tendering (204), freight invoicing (210), shipment tracking (214), rail transport (404), warehouse operations (945), payment remittance (820), price catalogs (832), and load tender response (990). Multi-version X12 (00204 through 005010) with multi-modal transportation.
demos.author: Delta Forge Team

demos.name: Excel Sales Analytics — Superstore Orders
demos.summary: Demonstrates multi-file Excel analytics using 4 years of Superstore sales data (2014–2017, 9,994 orders). Two tables exercise sheet_name, file_filter, infer_schema_rows, multi-file reading, file_metadata, and automatic type inference for dates, numbers, and strings.
demos.author: Delta Forge Team

demos.name: Excel Multi-Sheet Reporting — Regional Sales Review
demos.summary: Demonstrates Excel multi-sheet reading using quarterly field-office reports from two regions (East and West). Each workbook contains three sheets — Sales transactions, Returns/refunds, and Staff roster. Creates one external table per sheet, then queries across sheets and regions with cross-sheet JOINs, net-revenue calculations, and return-rate analysis. This scenario is unique to Excel — no other format has named sheets.
demos.author: Delta Forge Team

demos.name: Excel Options Testbench — Connector Validation
demos.summary: Verifies that each Excel parsing option is correctly wired through to the backend. Each table exercises a specific option (sheet_name, has_header, skip_rows, max_rows, file_filter) with data designed so that incorrect parsing produces obviously wrong results. Includes a summary query that reports PASS/FAIL for each option.
demos.author: Delta Forge Team

demos.name: FHIR Clinical Observations — Vital Signs & Lab Results
demos.summary: Demonstrates FHIR R5 Observation resource ingestion across vital signs (body weight, height, BMI, blood pressure, temperature, SpO2), lab results (glucose, CO2, hemoglobin), and clinical assessments (Glasgow Coma Scale). 100 bulk heart rate readings plus 14 individual clinical observations. Covers deep nested JSON flattening, schema evolution across observation types, and multi-file reading.
demos.author: Delta Forge Team

demos.name: FHIR Clinical Records — Conditions, Procedures & Allergies
demos.summary: Demonstrates advanced FHIR R5 resource ingestion across three clinical resource types: 8 Condition resources (diagnoses with SNOMED CT coding, severity, body site), 8 Procedure resources (surgeries with performers and outcomes), and 6 AllergyIntolerance resources (allergies with reaction history and criticality). Three tables showcase multi-resource-type directory scanning, deep nested arrays, and cross-resource references.
demos.author: Delta Forge Team

demos.name: FHIR Medications — Prescriptions & Coverage
demos.summary: Demonstrates FHIR R5 MedicationRequest and Coverage resource ingestion. 12 prescriptions with contained Medication resources, dosage instructions, dispense requests, and substitution rules. 4 Coverage resources with insurance classes and beneficiary references. Covers json_paths for preserving complex subtrees, contained resources, and deep FHIR nesting.
demos.author: Delta Forge Team

demos.name: FHIR Patient Demographics — Basics
demos.summary: Demonstrates FHIR R5 Patient resource ingestion from both bulk NDJSON exports (50 patients) and individual JSON files (7 detailed patients). Two tables show flat bulk demographics vs rich nested FHIR data with names, telecom, addresses, and managing organizations. Covers NDJSON format, column_mappings, schema evolution, and file_metadata.
demos.author: Delta Forge Team

demos.name: FHIR XML — Clinical Patients & Observations
demos.summary: Demonstrates HL7 FHIR R4 clinical data in native XML format. FHIR XML stores all primitive values as @value attributes. 8 Patient and 8 Observation XML files showcase FHIR namespace handling, @value extraction, exclude_paths for narrative XHTML, xml_paths for component/referenceRange subtrees, repeating element join_comma, deep XPath column_mappings, and schema evolution.
demos.author: Delta Forge Team

demos.name: GIS Emergency Response Network — Multi-Step Spatial Analytics
demos.summary: Demonstrates complex multi-step GIS analytics using an NYC emergency dispatch scenario. 8 hospitals, 12 incidents, and 4 response zones exercise nearest-neighbor ranking (ROW_NUMBER + st_distance), multi-algorithm distance comparison (haversine/sphere/Vincenty), navigation bearings, geofencing (st_contains/st_within with zone polygons), coordinate round-trips, and geodesic area calculations in layered analytical queries.
demos.author: Delta Forge Team

demos.name: GIS Maritime Shipping — PostGIS-Compatible Geospatial Functions
demos.summary: Tests all 18 PostGIS-compatible st_* functions using a global maritime shipping scenario. 5 cargo vessels navigate between 10 world ports with 40 GPS position records. Exercises st_distance (haversine, Vincenty, spherical), st_bearing, st_azimuth, st_final_bearing, st_contains, st_within, st_make_point, st_x, st_y, and st_area with independently computed proof values.
demos.author: Delta Forge Team

demos.name: Graph Cypher Queries — Pattern Matching & Algorithms
demos.summary: Cypher query language demo on a 5-person social graph. Pattern matching (MATCH, WHERE, RETURN), variable-length paths, and 15+ graph algorithm procedures: PageRank, betweenness, closeness, degree centrality, connected components, Louvain community detection, triangle counting, shortest path, BFS/DFS, MST, and KNN similarity.
demos.author: Delta Forge Team

demos.name: Dolphins Social Network — Marine Biology Graph
demos.summary: Social associations between 62 bottlenose dolphins in Doubtful Sound, New Zealand (Lusseau et al., 2003). 159 undirected edges. Known community structure, triangle count (285), and max degree (12).
demos.author: Delta Forge Team

demos.name: Email-Eu-core — European Research Institution
demos.summary: Directed email communication network from a European research institution (SNAP). 1,005 persons, 25,571 directed edges. 42 ground-truth department communities. 10 weakly connected components, 105,461 triangles, diameter 7.
demos.author: Delta Forge Team

demos.name: Graph Flattened Mode — Direct Column Access
demos.summary: Demonstrates the flattened property storage mode for graph tables. All vertex and edge properties stored as individual columns — fastest mode with full predicate pushdown. 5-person social graph with 6 directed friendships. Queries cover traversal, aggregation, 2-hop neighbors, reciprocal edges, and department connectivity.
demos.author: Delta Forge Team

demos.name: Graph Hybrid Mode — Columns + JSON Extras
demos.summary: Demonstrates the hybrid property storage mode for graph tables. Frequently queried properties as columns for fast access, optional/extensible properties in a JSON extras column. Same 5-person social graph showing mixed column + JSON access patterns and the balanced trade-off between performance and flexibility.
demos.author: Delta Forge Team

demos.name: Graph JSON Mode — Schema-Free Properties
demos.summary: Demonstrates the JSON property storage mode for graph tables. All vertex and edge properties in a single JSON string column — most flexible mode for schema-free evolution. Same 5-person social graph with JSON extraction functions (json_get_str, json_get_int, json_get_float), dynamic property discovery, and array access.
demos.author: Delta Forge Team

demos.name: Zachary's Karate Club — Classic Graph Benchmark
demos.summary: The most studied graph in network science (Zachary, 1977). 34 club members, 78 undirected edges. Ground-truth 2-faction split for community detection verification. Known centrality values, degree distribution, and connected component structure.
demos.author: Delta Forge Team

demos.name: LDBC Social Network Benchmark — Full Model Graph Verification
demos.summary: Full LDBC Social Network Benchmark (SF0.1) with 8 entity types, 23 relationship types, and ~1.2M rows. Verifies graph algorithms (PageRank, shortest path, Louvain, centrality, BFS) and Cypher/SQL queries against official LDBC golden values. Demonstrates mixed SQL + Cypher queries joining graph algorithm results with Delta tables.
demos.author: Delta Forge Team

demos.name: NetScience — Coauthorship Network
demos.summary: Coauthorship network of 1,461 network-science researchers with 2,742 weighted edges (Newman, 2006). Multiple disconnected components, non-uniform weights, and modularity ~0.95 reflecting clear research-group structure.
demos.author: Delta Forge Team

demos.name: Political Books — Partisan Co-Purchasing Network
demos.summary: 105 US political books with 441 co-purchasing edges from Amazon (Krebs/Newman). Ground-truth 3-community partition (liberal/neutral/conservative). Max degree 25, avg clustering ~0.488.
demos.author: Delta Forge Team

demos.name: Graph Social Network — 100-Node Company Graph
demos.summary: Large-scale graph analytics on a 100-employee company social network with 300+ directed connections across 8 departments and 5 cities. Deterministic generate_series() data generation. Queries cover degree centrality, department cross-pollination, influence scoring, 2-hop neighborhoods, team clustering, bridge detection, and mentor network analysis.
demos.author: Delta Forge Team

demos.name: Graph Stress Test — 1M-Node Performance Benchmark
demos.summary: Extreme-scale graph performance benchmark: 1,000,000 vertex nodes and 5,000,000+ directed edges across 20 departments, 15 cities, and 200 project teams. Stress-tests graph rendering, layout algorithms, Cypher engine algorithms (PageRank, Louvain, BFS, shortest path, MST), and memory handling at massive scale.
demos.author: Delta Forge Team

demos.name: H3+GIS Delivery Optimization — Cross-Function Spatial Analytics
demos.summary: Combines H3 hexagonal indexing with GIS geospatial functions for US regional delivery logistics. 3 warehouses serve 15 stores with cross-function queries: H3 cell assignment + GIS distance ranking, grid topology + haversine comparison, multi-resolution hierarchy + bearing calculations, and suboptimal warehouse assignment detection using combined H3 grid_distance and st_distance metrics.
demos.author: Delta Forge Team

demos.name: H3 GPS Fleet Tracker
demos.summary: Demonstrates H3 hexagonal spatial indexing with Delta tables for GPS fleet tracking. Creates 10,000 GPS pings across 5 world cities, defines region boundaries as WKT polygons, and performs O(1) spatial joins via H3 cell matching. Verifies 21 H3 functions with known values: coordinate conversion, grid topology (ring/disk counts), cell hierarchy, area/edge metrics, polyfill, and spatial join accuracy.
demos.author: Delta Forge Team

demos.name: H3 Point-in-Polygon — 1M Row Performance
demos.summary: Demonstrates blazing-fast point-in-polygon queries using H3 hexagonal spatial indexing. 1,000,000 ride-share driver GPS positions are checked against 12 pricing zones across 8 world cities using O(1) H3 cell matching instead of O(n×m) polygon intersection. Progressive queries build from 'what is H3' to million-row analytics with 10 automated PASS/FAIL checks.
demos.author: Delta Forge Team

demos.name: HL7 Clinical Workflows — Documents, Scheduling & Edge Cases
demos.summary: Demonstrates HL7 v2 clinical workflow messages beyond core ADT and lab types. MDM^T02 document management with full History & Physical narrative, SIU^S12 appointment scheduling from two systems, and comprehensive edge-case testing with escape sequences (\F\, \T\, \S\, \R\, \E\, \X0D\), empty fields, and multi-line text.
demos.author: Delta Forge Team

demos.name: HL7 Lab Orders & Results — ORM/ORU Workflow
demos.summary: Models the complete laboratory workflow using HL7 v2 ORM (Order) and ORU (Observation Result) messages. 3 ORM orders and 5 ORU results from 4+ LIS/EHR systems across HL7 versions 2.3–2.5.1. Covers materialized PID/OBR/OBX extraction, value type handling (NM, ST, TX, SN), abnormal flag detection, escape sequence decoding, and cross-system unification.
demos.author: Delta Forge Team

demos.name: HL7 Patient Administration — ADT Lifecycle
demos.summary: Demonstrates HL7 v2 ADT (Admit-Discharge-Transfer) messages from 6 EHR systems across 5 HL7 versions (2.3–2.6). 8 real-world messages cover admission (A01), demographics update (A08), and discharge (A03). Features MSH header extraction, materialized PID/PV1/EVN paths, escape sequence decoding, Z-segment preservation, and cross-EHR field mapping.
demos.author: Delta Forge Team

demos.name: Iceberg Energy Grid — Native Table Reading
demos.summary: Demonstrates native Apache Iceberg table reading using a smart energy grid monitoring dataset (600 meter readings across 3 regions). Exercises Iceberg metadata chain parsing (metadata.json → manifest list → manifest → Parquet data files), schema inference from Iceberg metadata, manifest-based file discovery, region-level aggregations, and Parquet row-group filtering — all without any conversion to Delta format.
demos.author: Delta Forge Team

demos.name: Iceberg Native — Time Travel (Stock Prices)
demos.summary: Demonstrates native Apache Iceberg V2 table reading after 4 snapshot-producing mutations on a stock price dataset (138 final rows, 23 tickers). Exercises merge-on-read with position delete files across UPDATE (+5% tech correction), INSERT (30 IPO rows), and DELETE (12 delisted rows). Verifies per-ticker averages, sector breakdowns, and absence of deleted data.
demos.author: Delta Forge Team

demos.name: Iceberg UniForm — Enable Dual-Format Metadata
demos.summary: Demonstrates the core Iceberg UniForm feature: enabling automatic Iceberg metadata generation on a Delta table by setting delta.universalFormat.enabledFormats = 'iceberg'. After enabling, every Delta commit produces Iceberg-compatible metadata (metadata.json, manifest lists, manifest files) alongside the Delta transaction log — making the table readable by any Iceberg engine without conversion. Uses a product catalog dataset to show property configuration, DML operations with dual metadata, and DESCRIBE commands.
demos.author: Delta Forge Team

demos.name: Iceberg UniForm — CRUD & Snapshot Lifecycle
demos.summary: Walks through the complete lifecycle of a UniForm-enabled Delta table: INSERT, UPDATE, DELETE operations, each producing a new Iceberg snapshot alongside the Delta version. Uses time travel to inspect data at each snapshot boundary. Demonstrates that Iceberg metadata stays in sync with the Delta log across all mutation types. Dataset: 20 employees across 4 departments.
demos.author: Delta Forge Team

demos.name: Iceberg UniForm — Schema Evolution Across Formats
demos.summary: Demonstrates schema evolution on a UniForm-enabled Delta table. When columns are added via ALTER TABLE ADD COLUMN, both the Delta schema and the Iceberg schema evolve in lockstep. Older snapshots reference the original schema; newer snapshots reference the evolved schema. Dataset: customer orders with progressive column additions for loyalty_tier, discount_pct, and notes.
demos.author: Delta Forge Team

demos.name: Iceberg UniForm — V1, V2, V3 Format Comparison
demos.summary: Compares Iceberg format versions V1, V2, and V3 side by side. Creates three identical Delta tables with the same seed data, each configured with a different delta.universalFormat.icebergVersion property. Demonstrates that all three versions produce correct metadata, handle DML, and support time travel — while V3 adds nanosecond timestamp types, deletion vectors, and named references.
demos.author: Delta Forge Team

demos.name: Iceberg UniForm — Partitioned Tables
demos.summary: Demonstrates Iceberg UniForm on a partitioned Delta table. The Iceberg metadata includes partition specs that map directly from the Delta partition columns, enabling Iceberg-compatible engines to leverage partition pruning. Shows INSERT, UPDATE, DELETE across partitions with time travel verification. Dataset: 24 sales transactions across 3 regions and 4 quarters.
demos.author: Delta Forge Team

demos.name: Customer Loyalty Program — Bloom Filters with UniForm
demos.summary: Tests BLOOM FILTER COLUMNS on a UniForm Iceberg table. 40 loyalty members across 4 tiers (Bronze/Silver/Gold/Platinum) exercise point lookups by member_id and full_name. Verifies bloom filter metadata coexists with UniForm Iceberg metadata and data fidelity through Iceberg read-back.
demos.author: Delta Forge Team

demos.name: E-Commerce Order Lifecycle — Change Data Feed with UniForm
demos.summary: Tests CDF (Change Data Feed) integration with UniForm Iceberg metadata. 30 e-commerce orders across 5 customers and 5 products undergo INSERT, UPDATE (pending to processing), and DELETE (cancelled orders). Proves CDF mutations are correctly reflected in the shadow Iceberg metadata chain.
demos.author: Delta Forge Team

demos.name: Bank Transaction Validation — CHECK Constraints with UniForm
demos.summary: Tests CHECK constraints (delta.constraints.*) on a UniForm Iceberg table. 25 bank transactions across 5 accounts and 3 currencies enforce positive amounts and valid currency codes. Verifies constraint metadata via SHOW TBLPROPERTIES and data integrity through Iceberg read-back.
demos.author: Delta Forge Team

demos.name: Regulatory Compliance Recovery — RESTORE with UniForm
demos.summary: Tests RESTORE TO VERSION on a UniForm Iceberg table. 20 compliance records are seeded, updated (non_compliant to under_review), then accidentally deleted. RESTORE recovers the pre-delete state and Iceberg read-back verifies the shadow metadata is rebuilt correctly after the restore operation.
demos.author: Delta Forge Team

demos.name: Regional Sales Performance — Window Analytics with UniForm
demos.summary: Tests window functions (ROW_NUMBER, RANK, LAG, LEAD, running totals) on a UniForm Iceberg table. 40 sales records across 7 reps, 4 regions, and 3 product categories exercise PARTITION BY, ORDER BY, and frame clauses. Iceberg read-back proves analytical results are identical through both metadata chains.
demos.author: Delta Forge Team

demos.name: Iceberg V1 — Warehouse Inventory Snapshots
demos.summary: Demonstrates native Apache Iceberg format-version 1 table reading using a retail warehouse inventory dataset (489 SKUs across 3 warehouses). Exercises v1 metadata chain parsing, schema inference, and inventory analytics.
demos.author: Delta Forge Team

demos.name: Iceberg V2 — Retail Multi-Dimensional Aggregation
demos.summary: Demonstrates multi-dimensional aggregate analytics on a native Iceberg V2 table. 120 retail transactions across 4 stores, 3 regions, and 5 categories exercise GROUPING SETS, ROLLUP, FILTER clause, DISTINCT aggregates, and HAVING predicates. Essential for OLAP-style reporting on Iceberg data without conversion.
demos.author: Delta Forge Team

demos.name: Iceberg V2 — Complex Types (E-Commerce Orders)
demos.summary: Demonstrates native Iceberg V2 reading of complex column types — STRUCT for shipping addresses and ARRAY<STRUCT> for order line items. 100 e-commerce orders exercise nested field access via dot notation, UNNEST for array explosion (311 line items), and aggregations on nested struct fields across 15 cities and 12 states.
demos.author: Delta Forge Team

demos.name: Iceberg V2 — Copy-on-Write (Logistics Shipments)
demos.summary: Demonstrates Iceberg V2 copy-on-write mode where UPDATE and DELETE operations rewrite entire data files instead of using delete files. 120 shipments undergo delivery status updates (+20 delivered) and cancellation removals (-10 deleted), producing 110 final rows across 3 snapshots with no position delete files.
demos.author: Delta Forge Team

demos.name: Iceberg V2 — Fleet Telemetry Analytics
demos.summary: Demonstrates native Apache Iceberg format-version 2 table reading using a commercial fleet telemetry dataset (450 GPS pings across 3 regional fleets). Exercises v2 metadata chain parsing with enhanced column statistics, safety analytics, and fuel monitoring.
demos.author: Delta Forge Team

demos.name: Iceberg V2 — Hidden Partitions (Ride-Share Analytics)
demos.summary: Demonstrates Iceberg V2 hidden partitioning using months(pickup_date) transform. 300 ride-share trip records across 6 monthly partitions (Jan-Jun 2025) exercise transparent partition pruning — queries filter on pickup_date without knowing partition structure. Tests multi-city analytics, payment type breakdowns, and driver performance metrics.
demos.author: Delta Forge Team

demos.name: Iceberg V2 — Multi-Column Partitioning (Weather Stations)
demos.summary: Demonstrates Iceberg V2 multi-column partitioning using region (identity) and years(observation_date) transforms. 450 weather station readings across 15 partitions (5 regions × 3 years) exercise compound partition pruning, per-station aggregation, year-over-year comparisons, and extreme reading detection.
demos.author: Delta Forge Team

demos.name: Iceberg V2 — Snapshot Consistency (Retail Inventory)
demos.summary: Demonstrates Iceberg V2 snapshot consistency with 4 mutations on a retail inventory table: initial load (80 products), restocking (+20 INSERTs), price corrections (UPDATE Electronics +8%), and discontinuation (-10 DELETEs). Final state of 90 products verifies merge-on-read with position delete files across all mutation types.
demos.author: Delta Forge Team

demos.name: Iceberg V2 — Airline Loyalty Window Analytics
demos.summary: Demonstrates advanced window function analytics on a native Iceberg V2 table. 60 frequent flyer members across 4 tiers and 5 airports exercise ROW_NUMBER, RANK, DENSE_RANK, LAG, LEAD, NTILE, and running SUM window functions. CTEs implement top-N-per-group patterns, proving the query engine handles complex analytical SQL on Iceberg data.
demos.author: Delta Forge Team

demos.name: Iceberg V3 — Clinical Trial Lab Results
demos.summary: Demonstrates native Apache Iceberg format-version 3 table reading using a pharmaceutical clinical trial dataset (480 lab results across 3 trial sites). Generated by Spark 4.0 + Iceberg 1.10.1. Exercises V3 metadata chain parsing, abnormal result detection, and analyst workload analysis.
demos.author: Delta Forge Team

demos.name: Iceberg V3 UniForm — CDF Payment Reconciliation
demos.summary: Tests that Change Data Feed (CDF) and Iceberg UniForm V3 coexist correctly on the same Delta table. 30 payment transactions across 5 merchants undergo batch approval, decline, fraud removal, and new payment insertion with both CDF change tracking and V3 Iceberg metadata generation active. Iceberg read-back verifies the V3 shadow metadata chain stays consistent through all mutations.
demos.author: Delta Forge Team

demos.name: Iceberg V3 — Clinical Lab NULL Edge Cases
demos.summary: Demonstrates NULL handling edge cases on a native Iceberg V3 table. 50 clinical lab results with intentional NULLs across 7 columns exercise COALESCE, NULLIF, COUNT(*) vs COUNT(column), GROUP BY with NULL buckets, CASE with NULLs, and aggregate behavior when NULLs are present. Proves V3 NULL statistics and predicate evaluation work correctly.
demos.author: Delta Forge Team

demos.name: JSON Country Factbook — Deep Nesting & Schema Evolution
demos.summary: Demonstrates deep JSON nesting (3+ levels), schema evolution with NULL filling, and selective path extraction using CIA World Factbook data for 10 African countries. One-object-per-file format with optional Terrorism/Space sections. Two tables: countries (flattened overview with preserve_original) and country_economy (GDP, sectors, trade). Covers exclude_paths, column_mappings, max_depth, and multi-file reading.
demos.author: Delta Forge Team

demos.name: JSON Customers — Basics
demos.summary: Demonstrates JSON fundamentals: creating an external table from a JSON array file, applying column_mappings for clean names ($.first → first_name, $.last → last_name), and using type inference for proper timestamp handling. 200 CRM customer records with include_paths, max_depth, and file_metadata.
demos.author: Delta Forge Team

demos.name: JSON Music Catalog — Nested Arrays & Explode
demos.summary: Demonstrates nested JSON flattening with array explosion. 347 music albums with nested vendor objects and track detail arrays across 3 genre-based catalog files. Two tables: album_tracks (exploded, 3,503 rows) and album_summary (347 rows with vendor as JSON blob). Covers explode_paths, json_paths, column_mappings, default_array_handling, and multi-file reading.
demos.author: Delta Forge Team

demos.name: JSON Subtree Capture — json_paths
demos.summary: Demonstrates json_paths for capturing complex JSON subtrees as serialized string columns instead of flattening. Two tables show the same property listing data: one with location and pricing subtrees captured as JSON blobs (json_paths), one fully flattened for comparison. Covers json_paths, column_mappings, include_paths, and multi-file reading.
demos.author: Delta Forge Team

demos.name: Northwind Trading Company
demos.summary: Classic relational database with customers, orders, products, employees, and suppliers. 13 interconnected tables demonstrating joins, aggregations, and business analytics.
demos.author: Delta Forge Team

demos.name: ORC Banking Transactions — Transaction Monitoring
demos.summary: Demonstrates ORC format reading with branch-level banking transaction records for fraud monitoring. Two ORC files (100 rows) across two bank branches exercise multi-file aggregation, flagged transaction filtering, risk score analysis, and per-account summaries with file_metadata for traceability.
demos.author: Delta Forge Team

demos.name: ORC Server Logs — HTTP Access Analytics
demos.summary: Demonstrates ORC reading features using 5 server access log files (2,500 rows) with schema evolution (v1→v2 adds request_body_bytes, cache_hit). Three tables exercise multi-file reading, file_filter for single-server extraction, max_rows for sampling, file_metadata, and ORC's self-describing schema with automatic type detection from file footers.
demos.author: Delta Forge Team

demos.name: Parquet Flight Delays — Airline Delay Analysis
demos.summary: Demonstrates Parquet schema evolution using 3 quarterly flight-delay files (120 rows). Q1 has base columns, Q2 adds delay_reason, Q3 adds carrier_code. Two tables exercise multi-file reading with automatic NULL filling for evolved columns, file_filter for single-quarter extraction, and file_metadata for source traceability.
demos.author: Delta Forge Team

demos.name: Parquet Supply Chain — Order Analytics
demos.summary: Demonstrates Parquet reading features using 14 quarterly order files (2012–2016, 73,089 rows) organized in year-based subdirectories. Four tables exercise recursive directory scanning, file_filter for year/quarter extraction, max_rows for sampling, row_group_filter for predicate pushdown, file_metadata for traceability, and Parquet's self-describing schema.
demos.author: Delta Forge Team

demos.name: Protobuf Address Book — Contact Directory
demos.summary: Demonstrates Protocol Buffers (proto3) binary file reading using the classic Google addressbook schema. 3 team roster files (13 contacts, 22 phone numbers) read into flattened contacts and exploded phone-number tables. Covers nested message flattening, repeated field handling (join vs. explode), enum decoding (PhoneType), well-known types (Timestamp to ISO 8601), sparse data, and column mappings.
demos.author: Delta Forge Team

demos.name: Protobuf Freight Shipping — Multi-Carrier Manifest
demos.summary: Tests protobuf features beyond existing demos: boolean fields (is_express, is_insured, requires_signature), int64 monetary values, float weights/dimensions, 3-level message nesting (Shipment → Package → Dimensions), multiple enums (ShipmentStatus, PackageClass), and multiple repeated fields per message. 3 carrier files contain 12 shipments, 24 packages, and 39 tracking events in a freight forwarding scenario.
demos.author: Delta Forge Team

demos.name: Protobuf IoT Sensor Network — Manufacturing Floor Sensors
demos.summary: Demonstrates Protobuf reading with 3 binary files (20 sensors, 82 readings) from a manufacturing IoT network. Two tables exercise different flatten configurations: exploded view (one row per reading) and summary view (one row per sensor with comma-joined readings). Tests nested messages, repeated fields, timestamp conversion, and multi-file reading.
demos.author: Delta Forge Team

demos.name: Sales Data Quickstart
demos.summary: Minimal sales dataset to get started quickly. Two CSV files showing basic queries, filtering, and aggregations.
demos.author: Delta Forge Team

demos.name: XML Books — Schema Evolution
demos.summary: Demonstrates XML schema evolution across 5 catalog export files spanning 2000–2004. Each year adds new elements (isbn, publisher, rating, edition, series) and one drops description. Verifies the union schema, NULL filling, and attribute extraction.
demos.author: Delta Forge Team

demos.name: XML E-Commerce — Order Line Explosion
demos.summary: Demonstrates deep XML nesting (3+ levels), explode_paths for one-row-per-line-item, CDATA sections, exclude_paths, column_mappings, and self-closing flag elements on e-commerce order batch data with 5 orders and 11 line items.
demos.author: Delta Forge Team

demos.name: XML NYT News — RSS Feed Analysis
demos.summary: Demonstrates XML namespace handling (4 namespaces: dc, media, atom, nyt), repeating element modes (JoinComma and Explode), self-closing element attributes, column_mappings, exclude_paths, and multi-file reading across 7 NYT RSS feed exports with 231 news articles and 2023 category keywords.
demos.author: Delta Forge Team

demos.name: XML Subtree Capture — xml_paths & nested_output_format
demos.summary: Demonstrates xml_paths for capturing complex XML subtrees as serialized string columns instead of flattening. Two tables show the same product catalog with subtrees (specifications, supplier) captured as JSON objects or raw XML fragments via nested_output_format. Top-level fields are flattened normally alongside captured subtrees.
demos.author: Delta Forge Team

demos.name: Delta Z-ORDER vs Partitioning — Choosing the Right Data Layout
demos.summary: The most common Delta table design question: should I partition or Z-ORDER? This demo builds the same e-commerce dataset in two tables — one partitioned by region, one Z-ORDERed — and runs side-by-side queries showing where each strategy wins.
demos.author: Delta Forge Team

demos.name: Pseudonymisation Healthcare — HIPAA/GDPR Multi-Format
demos.summary: Comprehensive GDPR-compliant pseudonymisation across three healthcare data formats: HL7 v2 ADT patient admissions (4 rows), FHIR R4 Patient resources (4 rows), and EDI HIPAA X12 claims (5 rows). Demonstrates all 7 transform types (keyed_hash, encrypt, redact, generalize, tokenize, mask, hash), wildcard column patterns (address_*, *_name), all 3 scopes (person, relationship, transaction), and 22 rules with aggregations on protected data.
demos.author: Delta Forge Team

demos.name: Pseudonymisation Quickstart — Banking KYC
demos.summary: A retail bank pseudonymises customer PII for its analytics team while keeping account-level aggregations accurate. Teaches the core concept: CREATE PSEUDONYMISATION RULE protects data at query time without modifying it on disk. Four transform types cover common KYC de-identification patterns: redact SSN, mask phone, keyed_hash names, and generalize dates of birth.
demos.author: Delta Forge Team

demos.name: Pseudonymisation Lifecycle — Insurance Claims
demos.summary: An insurance company manages pseudonymisation rules across the claims processing lifecycle. Rules are created for routine analytics, selectively dropped when no longer needed, and verified through filtered SHOW commands. Demonstrates rule lifecycle management: SHOW filtered views, DROP individual rules, and catalog propagation.
demos.author: Delta Forge Team

demos.name: Pseudonymisation Apply — Clinical Trial De-identification
demos.summary: A pharmaceutical company de-identifies clinical trial participant data for external research sharing. Demonstrates query-time pseudonymisation across two trials (6 participants) with SSN redaction, name hashing, and email masking. Aggregations by treatment arm and enrollment status work on protected data, proving analytics remain accurate. Covers GDPR Article 17 right-to-erasure compliance patterns.
demos.author: Delta Forge Team
